decisions.relationship_evidence: The study formulates a deterministic COVID-19 transmission model with direct and environmental transmission and analyzes its reproduction number and dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops and analyzes a transmission-model formulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes an eight-state generalized mechanistic epidemic model and evaluates its fitting and forecasting performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit methodological development and validation source for a mechanistic multi-state epidemic model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study introduces a novel COVID-19 transmission model incorporating airborne virus and direct-contact transmission, then fits and analyzes it.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly introduces and mathematically develops a new transmission-model structure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The model predicts grapevine infection from weather, phenology, and pathogen processes, but the text does not describe a compartmental transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is not an explicit lead for the exact compartmental-transmission concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Disease-transmission models are used metaphorically to represent noninfectious snake-human contact, without an explicit compartmental infectious-transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record is not a genuine lineage source for the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper develops a weather-driven process model of fungal epidemic development, but does not identify or use a compartmental transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The mechanistic process model is neighboring rather than exact-concept evidence.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes a susceptible-exposed-infected-removed-type model with time-varying rates and accompanying calibration and forecasting procedures.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a compartmental epidemic model and forecasting methodology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops and simulates an age- and nutrition-stratified population-level compartmental measles-transmission model to assess interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete application of compartmental transmission modeling to measles policy scenarios.
supported_label_variants: population-level, compartmental model of measles transmission

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors present a mechanistic epidemic model of intermediate complexity, infer time-varying transmission, and evaluate out-of-sample projections.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The abstract explicitly presents the modeling approach as a methodological bridge between empirical forecasts and scenario models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops and analyzes a compartment-structured HIV-HCV coinfection model using differential equations, reproduction numbers, equilibria, and stability analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies compartmental epidemic modeling to HIV-HCV coinfection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study evaluates and compares SIR, SEIR, and human-vector compartmental models combined with particle swarm optimization for estimating dengue R0.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit comparative evaluation of compartmental-model alternatives in an infectious-disease application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes a new differential-equation mechanistic model linking acute COVID-19 transmission, vaccination, and long-COVID dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops and validates a new mechanistic transmission-model framework.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A semi-mechanistic vaccine-efficacy model is integrated within a malaria transmission model to estimate health impact and cost-effectiveness.
decisions.lineage_evidence: This is a concrete malaria-vaccine application of mechanistic transmission modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Deterministic ordinary-differential-equation transmission models with vector, reservoir-host, and seasonal components are fitted to Ross River virus surveillance data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies mechanistic transmission models to explain Ross River virus dynamics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops, validates, and simulates an improved SEIR model to assess COVID-19 infections and testing requirements across scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete compartmental-model application for COVID-19 testing policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A multi-age susceptible-exposed-infected-hospitalized-recovered model with social contacts is fitted to COVID-19 surveillance data and analyzed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies an explicitly compartmental transmission model to the Fuzhou COVID-19 wave.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper constructs and fits an HIV/AIDS epidemic model, calculating equilibria, reproduction numbers, stability, and intervention effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The model is concretely applied to HIV/AIDS transmission data and prevention scenarios.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors use a compartmentalized, risk-structured, two-route mathematical transmission model to estimate mpox reproduction numbers and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete application of a compartmental transmission model to the mpox outbreak.
supported_label_variants: compartmentalized, risk-structured and two-route mathematical model

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study introduces a novel six-compartment epidemiological model and analyzes interventions, transmission, and underreporting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly introduces and develops a new compartmental transmission-model structure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper describes a novel discrete-time fitting method for recovering parameters of the SIR dynamic epidemic model and diagnoses weak identification.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit methodological source for fitting and identifying compartmental epidemic models.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The mechanistic immune-landscape model predicts variant susceptibility and fitness, but the abstract does not describe a compartmental transmission model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is not an explicit lineage lead for the exact compartmental-transmission concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The model explains temperature-driven tick questing phenology rather than infectious transmission between compartments.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The mechanistic phenology model is outside the exact concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The proposed mechanistic model concerns solids partitioning and physical virus removal in wastewater, not compartmental epidemic transmission.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is not a lineage source for the exact transmission-model concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A deterministic compartmental model calibrated to rat, flea, serological, and human surveillance data is used to analyze plague transmission and interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete compartmental-model application to plague seasonality and control.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A spatial SEIR model is developed and used to simulate SARS-CoV-2 spread and containment strategies in South Africa.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies a compartmental transmission model to pandemic policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study uses a two-population ordinary-differential-equation mechanistic model to infer within- and cross-population COVID-19 transmission rates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies mechanistic transmission modeling to university-community spread.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian framework fits a deterministic transmission model to case and surveillance data to characterize China's Omicron wave.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete application of deterministic epidemic transmission modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors develop and calibrate a multi-host mechanistic transmission model for African swine fever and evaluate control scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Although the model is developed explicitly, it is principally a concrete African swine fever application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A mechanistic SARS-CoV-2 model incorporating immunity and vaccination is calibrated to serological data to estimate underreporting and intervention effects.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete application to Omicron transmission in Newfoundland and Labrador.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper develops a compartmental C. difficile transmission model and introduces a colonization amplification index alongside the reproduction number.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a compartmental model and a new model-derived transmission metric.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A fitted mechanistic transmission model supplies a reference PAF used to evaluate three conventional population-attribution methods.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record uses an explicit mechanistic transmission model to validate and diagnose competing attribution methods.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A deterministic compartmental model is used to examine competing explanations for declining HIV reports among people who inject drugs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete HIV-policy application of compartmental modeling.

decisions.relationship_evidence: An age- and sex-structured compartmental chlamydia-transmission model is calibrated, validated, and used for vaccination and cost-effectiveness simulations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete compartmental-model application to chlamydia vaccination policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The validated ARAMIS HIV microsimulation model predicts clinical and economic outcomes for maraviroc treatment strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies microsimulation to HIV treatment cost-effectiveness.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A calibrated multi-HPV-type microsimulation model evaluates vaccination and cervical-screening strategies in Spain.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete HPV-policy application of microsimulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study performs microsimulation with a calibrated Markov model to compare HPV vaccination and screening strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies microsimulation and a decision-analytic Markov model to HPV policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A computer model projects lifetime HIV risk, life expectancy, costs, and cost-effectiveness under alternative pre-exposure prophylaxis strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete decision-analytic HIV-prevention application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: An individual-level micro-simulation model compares antiretroviral-access scenarios and projects epidemiologic and macroeconomic outcomes.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies microsimulation to HIV treatment policy in Africa.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A mathematical decision model compares HIV treatment-as-prevention, circumcision, and treatment strategies by infections, deaths, and costs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete HIV prevention and economic-policy modeling application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The ONCHOSIM micro-simulation model simulates infection and disease outcomes to estimate the health impact and costs of onchocerciasis control.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies microsimulation to an infectious-disease control program.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A transmission model incorporating a validated microsimulation HIV progression model evaluates portfolios of prevention strategies and costs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies microsimulation-informed decision modeling to HIV prevention planning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A micro-simulation model evaluates retention-in-care interventions and earlier treatment strategies in East African HIV programs.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete HIV-program application of microsimulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Markov micro-simulation model evaluates the cost-effectiveness of three hepatitis C treatment regimens.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concretely applies Markov microsimulation to HCV treatment decisions.

decisions.relationship_evidence: An individual-level micro-simulation model estimates QALYs, costs, and cost-effectiveness for HIV adherence interventions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete HIV-intervention application of microsimulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: An individual-based Markov micro-simulation model estimates tuberculosis cases under alternative latent-TB testing recommendations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies Markov microsimulation to tuberculosis screening policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Markov micro-simulation model simulates a prison cohort and compares the cost-effectiveness of hepatitis C screening strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete HCV-screening application of microsimulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: An open-cohort micro-simulation model projects HPV-related oropharyngeal cancer under vaccination-uptake scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies microsimulation to HPV vaccination and disease-burden planning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Monte Carlo simulation model tracks cow-level infection dynamics and costs to evaluate chronic mastitis on dairy farms.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete individual-level infectious-disease and economic simulation application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A micro-simulation COVID-19 transmission model is combined with willingness-to-travel and economic models to compare border policies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies microsimulation and decision analysis to COVID-19 travel policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A micro-simulation model predicts imported and secondary COVID-19 cases within a cost-benefit comparison of border policies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete COVID-19 policy application of microsimulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The title and correction notice identify only a generic simulation study and do not explicitly establish that the corrected article used microsimulation or a decision-analytic model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The exact concept and therefore an exact-concept correction lineage are unsupported.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study models and compares the expected benefits, risks, timing, uptake, and costs of challenge versus traditional hepatitis C vaccine trials.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete decision-analytic application to HCV vaccine-development policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A micro-simulation Markov model compares cervical-screening strategies under varying HPV prevalence and uptake.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies microsimulation and Markov decision analysis to HPV screening policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Three calibrated models simulate vaccination-defined cohorts and compare 92 cervical-screening strategies on a cost-effectiveness frontier.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete comparative decision-modeling application to HPV screening.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper develops a computational framework and site-placement algorithms for sizing sentinel networks, evaluated through simulated influenza spread.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit methodological source for simulation-based sentinel-network design.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops and evaluates sentinel-selection strategies for early outbreak detection on temporal networks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly addresses methodological optimization of sentinels in temporal-network epidemiology.
supported_label_variants: optimizing sentinel surveillance in temporal network epidemiology

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study compares three objective measures for optimizing network sentinels using epidemic models on static and temporal contact networks.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly evaluates how candidate optimization objectives alter sentinel rankings.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulated outbreaks on empirical and synthetic networks are used to derive and evaluate sentinel-node selection strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit methods source for network-based sentinel optimization.
supported_label_variants: sentinel surveillance strategies for preventing epidemics on networks

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study simulates outpatient sentinel sampling effort with a compartmental model but does not optimize sentinel placement on a contact or mobility network.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It concerns a neighboring sentinel-surveillance design rather than the exact network-optimization concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study implements linear optimization of sentinel-site allocation using Indigenous-region representation and national mobility-network coverage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops and validates a network-informed sentinel-allocation method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: This review compares healthcare-associated-infection benchmarking and public-reporting systems and identifies unresolved methodological issues and weak outcome evidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit review and interpretive guidance source for infection-surveillance benchmarking.
supported_label_variants: benchmarking of surveillance data for health-care-associated infection

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors present a case-mix adjustment and indirect-standardization model for risk-adjusted interhospital antibiotic-use rankings.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a risk-adjusted benchmarking method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The review describes methods for risk-adjusted antimicrobial-use benchmarking and critically assesses their advantages and remaining challenges.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is explicit methodological review and guidance for risk-adjusted benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study directly evaluates standardized infection-ratio models and argues that perioperative factors should not be used as predictive adjustment variables.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It empirically diagnoses a methodological limitation in risk-standardized infection benchmarking.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes an original hierarchical and Bayesian method for benchmarking adjusted regional healthcare-associated-infection prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a risk-adjusted benchmarking method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article describes available HAI benchmarks and explains confounding, risk adjustment, comparability, and interpretation challenges.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is explicit guidance on selecting and interpreting infection-surveillance benchmarks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors develop benchmarked charts and a principal-component dashboard for feeding comparative HIV audit performance back to clinics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a benchmarking and quality-feedback method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study describes and evaluates a parsimonious risk-adjusted observed-to-expected antibiotic-exposure ratio for interfacility benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a risk-standardized benchmarking metric.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Two risk-adjusted standardized-infection-ratio models are compared to assess feasibility and predictors of hospital-onset bacteremia benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly evaluates alternative risk-adjustment models and benchmarking feasibility.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The collaborative develops and deploys a multicenter benchmarking platform for pediatric outpatient antibiotic-use metrics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly provides an operational benchmarking platform and baseline metrics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study applies standardized mortality and resource-use rates to compare an infectious-diseases ICU with other ICU groups.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete infectious-disease quality-benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study benchmarks emergency-department blood-culture quality, identifies suboptimal collection groups, and proposes quality standards.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly evaluates performance against quality benchmarks and identifies deficiencies.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study uses a national benchmarking collaborative to compare pediatric ambulatory antibiotic prescribing before and during a shortage.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete antimicrobial-use benchmarking application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study develops a severity-adjusted hospital benchmarking report using observed-to-expected ratios for infection and antibiotic utilization.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a risk-adjusted comparative-performance method.
supported_label_variants: severity-adjusted, hospital-level benchmarking comparative performance report

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study compares antimicrobial-use/resistance ratios with use-only metrics and tests their interpretability for stewardship decisions.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly evaluates the utility of alternative benchmarking metrics.
supported_label_variants: benchmarking antimicrobial use to antimicrobial resistance

decisions.relationship_evidence: The authors develop a risk-adjusted smoothed observed-to-expected ratio for benchmarking broad-spectrum antibiotic use across hospitals.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit methodological development of a risk-standardized benchmarking measure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A multi-pathogen quantitative microbial risk-assessment framework estimates waterborne gastrointestinal disease burden and informs surveillance priorities.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete simulation-based burden-estimation and policy-planning application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study adapts a risk-assessment model within a cost-benefit analysis of five Salmonella-control strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies decision-analytic modeling to infectious-disease control policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Multiple burden models and an ensemble of transmission models estimate dengue burden and the impact of nationwide Wolbachia deployment.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete simulation-model application for burden estimation and intervention planning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A SEIR-based simulator projects infections, deaths, hospital demand, and intervention scenarios for COVID-19 in Turkey.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It applies simulation modeling to epidemic burden and health-system planning.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A dynamic transmission-based cost-effectiveness model estimates disease burden, health outcomes, and economic effects of infant meningococcal vaccination.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete vaccine-policy and burden-planning model application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Two decision-tree models with Markov elements compare maternal pertussis vaccination strategies and examine structural uncertainty.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly applies decision-analytic models to vaccination policy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Monte Carlo simulation-based quantitative microbial risk assessment estimates waterborne gastrointestinal illness and treatment-failure scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a concrete simulation-based disease-burden and water-safety planning application.
supported_label_variants: Monte Carlo simulation-based quantitative microbial risk assessment

decisions.relationship_evidence: Five outbreak-detection algorithms are compared on semi-synthetic datasets containing simulated outbreak signals after parameter optimization.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly benchmarks method performance using simulated outbreak data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes a diversity index for model-averaging model-space selection but does not describe a simulation-based method comparison or benchmark.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is not an exact-concept lineage lead.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The FlowCAP benchmark compares algorithms on a clinical cohort and blinded test set, without simulation-based evaluation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The benchmark is empirical rather than an explicit simulation-based method comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The simulation study compares two families of contact-network models by their ability to reproduce network features and simulated SIR epidemic dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly evaluates competing methods through epidemic simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study benchmarks resistance-gene methods on observed bacterial genome datasets and down-sampled reads, not simulated comparison data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is a computational benchmark but not an explicit simulation-based benchmark.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposes temporal knockout scores as a benchmark for evaluating network-influence measures using SIR and SIS propagation dynamics.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly develops a simulation-grounded benchmark measure for method evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The proposed performance-improvement model combines process control and benchmarking of observed indicators, without simulation-based method comparison.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It addresses quality benchmarking rather than the exact simulation-comparison concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The benchmark dataset is laboratory-generated with a known experimental phylogeny rather than produced through simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It does not explicitly support the simulation-based comparison concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Eleven metagenomic classifiers are evaluated using laboratory-generated and simulated controls across 846 species.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly benchmarks method performance with simulated as well as experimental controls.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The article reviews Lyme serodiagnostic assays and calls two-tier testing a benchmark, but contains no simulation-based method comparison.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The benchmark language is unrelated to the exact simulation-method concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Sequencing methods are validated against artificial bacterial control samples and patient sputum, without simulated evaluation data.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an empirical validation benchmark rather than an explicit simulation-based comparison.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Four genomic resistance-prediction tools are benchmarked on isolates from existing databases, not through simulation.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The evaluation does not support the exact simulation-based benchmarking concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Two simulation studies compare Kaplan-Meier and cumulative-incidence approaches and associated tests under competing-risk scenarios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It explicitly diagnoses statistical-method performance through simulation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Two simulations evaluate dynamic Bayesian networks against Granger causality and LASSO and assess forecasting performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an explicit simulation-based comparative performance evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: More than 70 tool-and-parameter combinations are benchmarked on 100 simulated datasets, and the top pipelines are supplied in the SEPATH tool.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The paper both performs simulation-based benchmarking and provides an implemented benchmark-derived pipeline.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study compares two surveillance data-collection methodologies using observed hospital data, without simulation-based benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is an empirical surveillance-method comparison rather than the exact simulation concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper applies and extends the benchmark-multiplier population-size estimator; 'benchmark' here is part of a distinct estimation method, not simulation-based benchmarking.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It is unrelated to the exact canonical concept.
